title: Agent evaluation measures the wrong variable
summary: For many agentic tasks, the relevant unit of evaluation is not the model alone but the workflow built around it.
Benchmark proximity can conceal large differences in cost, recoverability, auditability, and long-horizon usefulness.
critique: Current evaluation practice often treats workflow, environment, and budget as nuisance variables, even when they are central to practical system quality.
Single-score benchmark culture overstates what model-only comparisons can tell us about real agent performance.
whoShouldCare: Researchers and builders evaluating agent systems, comparing open and closed model deployments, or using benchmark scores as proxies for real-world usefulness.
openQuestions: Which workflow-level metrics best capture recoverability, auditability, and robustness without collapsing back into vague scoreboards?
On long-horizon tool-using tasks, how often does workflow variation dominate modest model variation?
hotTake: In agent systems, workflow variation is often treated as noise when it may be the main variable worth measuring.
